prompt:
Use case: stylized-concept
Asset type: project-bound RPG world atlas map, portrait orientation
Primary request: Call of Cthulhu / 1920s Lovecraftian inspired cosmic horror atlas for the terrain type diverse_archipelago.
Scene/backdrop: a vast cold ocean world of many island groups and microcontinents, New England-style fishing towns, Arkham-like university islands, Miskatonic river deltas, fog-shrouded rocky hills, old colonial harbors, lighthouse coastlines, cult-haunted villages, forbidden standing stones, marsh atolls, shipwreck reefs, remote polar isles and strange ruins beneath ordinary 1920s settlements.
Style/medium: high-resolution illustrated 1920s investigative atlas, aged parchment with hand annotations, sepia ink, fog and damp paper texture, creeping cosmic dread, occult marginalia, subtle impossible geometry.
Composition/framing: tall portrait world map with unevenly distributed island clusters; include large island regions, tiny haunted islets, crescent island arcs, black lagoons, reef belts, narrow straits, remote atolls, fragmented cliffs and microcontinents with no grid-like spacing; include compass rose, ship routes, rail-route fragments, lighthouse icons, standing stone symbols and investigator annotations without readable text.
Geography constraints: islands must vary strongly in size and shape, with realistic complex coastlines, broken shores, curved bays, peninsulas, foggy deltas, salt marshes, dark estuaries, cliff coasts and storm harbors; show many climates and regions including rocky coasts, dark forests, rural farmlands, mountains, wetlands, cold islands, moors, polar wastes and uncanny ruined zones.
Avoid: evenly spaced islands, heroic fantasy, visible tentacle monster centerpiece, cheerful tropical palette, modern satellite image, clean tourist map, readable labels, UI, watermark, logo, real-world map copy.
